title: A Rare Case of 10-year-old Child with Multisystem Inflammatory Syndrome (MIS-C)
author: Ashish Goti
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: 10-year-old male presented with 5 days of fever accompanied with cough, vomiting, muscle weakness, conjunctival injection, tachycardia and pharyngeal erythema alert but having toxic look. Detailed laboratory investigations revealed MIS-C with no covid contact history and RT PCR were negative. Dobutamine and methylprednisolone led to improved therapeutic outcome.
steps_raw: 10-year-old male presented with 5 days of fever accompanied with cough, vomiting, muscle weakness, conjunctival injection, tachycardia and pharyngeal erythema alert but having toxic look. Detailed laboratory investigations revealed MIS-C with no covid contact history and RT PCR were negative. Dobutamine and methylprednisolone led to improved therapeutic outcome.
keywords: MIS-C; Pediatric Patient; Post Covid Illness.
